topic: A person runs into their ex at a mutual friend's wedding
raw_output:
[story 1]

The ballroom was a dizzying kaleidoscope of silk and forced merriment. I clutched my champagne flute like a lifeline, trying to appear engaged in the conversation about the canapés, but my eyes kept snagging on a familiar silhouette near the punch bowl. Then, the music seemed to dim, and the air around me grew thick, heavy. It was him. Mark. He was standing near the entrance, laughing with the groom, and for a sickening moment, time seemed to warp. My breath hitched, a sharp, shallow intake that felt like sandpaper in my chest. Every muscle in my body locked up; my hands started to sweat, slick and cold against the crystal. I desperately wanted to melt into the wallpaper, to vanish. My gaze snapped away, forcing a strained smile onto my face as I navigated toward the refreshment table, every step feeling impossibly loud on the polished floor.

[story 2]

I had been trying to orchestrate a graceful exit from the crowded reception, seeking refuge near the terrace doors, when the recognition hit me like a physical blow. Standing by the string lights, bathed in the warm glow of the evening, was Sarah. My stomach plummeted straight to my ankles. A cold wave washed over me, stealing the warmth from my cheeks. I froze mid-stride, my carefully rehearsed pleasantries evaporating. My throat tightened, making it difficult to draw a full breath. I could feel a frantic drumming beneath my ribs, a chaotic rhythm that drowned out the ambient chatter of the party. I gripped the railing until my knuckles were white, focusing solely on the distant, dark line of the cityscape, wishing with every fiber of my being that the ground would simply open up and swallow me whole.

[story 3]

The noise of the wedding—the laughter, the clinking glasses, the upbeat band—suddenly became an unbearable assault. I was talking to a colleague, nodding along mechanically, when I saw her across the room. Elena. She looked radiant, impossibly composed, and the sight of her triggered an immediate, visceral reaction. My vision narrowed, the edges of the room blurring into streaks of color. A strange numbness spread through my limbs, making it hard to move, as if I were wading through deep water. I managed to pull my attention away by pretending to adjust my dress, but my hands trembled uncontrollably. I felt a desperate urge to flee, to bolt out the nearest exit and disappear into the anonymity of the night, my heart hammering against my ribs with a frantic, uneven beat.
